memoryArtifacts.headline: Lady Margaret Wyndham Luttrell Biography
memoryArtifacts.description:
Daughter of Sir Thomas Wyndham of Felbrigg by his first wife Lady Alianor Scrope Wyndham.

Wife of Sir Andrew Luttrell of Dunster Castle and East Quantoxhead, who she survived for 42 years.

Dunster P …

memoryArtifacts.description: History: Probably made for Dunster Castle in Somerset, or East Quantoxhead Hall, Somerset, seats of the Luttrell family, feudal barons of Dunster. Showing in its centre the arms of Luttrell impaling W …

Wife of Sir Andrew Luttrell of Dunster Castle and East Quantoxhead, who she survived for 42 years.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Wyndham於1501年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾時，她的父親Sir Thomas Wyndham33歲，母親Eleanor Scrope29歲。她於1514年4月22日與Andrew Luttrell在英國英格蘭薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1580年7月7日在英國薩默塞特易亞斯特 全藍托潔黑阿德享年79歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特易亞斯特 全藍托潔黑阿德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Wyndham于1501年出生在英国英格兰越可爾时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Wyndham33岁，母亲Eleanor Scrope29岁。她于1514年4月22日与Andrew Luttrell在英国英格兰薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1580年7月7日在英国薩默塞特易亞斯特 全藍托潔黑阿德享年79岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特易亞斯特 全藍托潔黑阿德。

nameMeanings.name: Wyndham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Windham  .

variants.name: Windham
meanings: English: habitational name usually from the Norfolk placename Wymondham, which is locally pronounced /wɪndəm/. The placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Wīgmund + Old English hām ‘village, homestead’. By various marriages in the 16th century the Wyndham family of Felbrigg Hall, Norfolk, acquired estates in North Yorkshire and Somerset, for which reason the manor of Orchard in the parish of Watchet, came to be known as Orchard Wyndham in the early 17th century. Later descendants became earls of Egremont, whose estates included Petworth in Sussex. This and other marital acquistions probably explain the post-medieval presence of the surname in Somerset, Devon, Dorset, Wiltshire, Gloucestershire, northern England, and perhaps also (from the late 17th century) in Sussex and Kent. In 1741 Percy Wyndham of the Orchard family inherited the earldom of Thomond, in Ireland, which may account for the currency of the name in Connaght (see 4 below).

English: habitational name perhaps occasionally from Wymondham in Leicestershire. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Wīgmund (genitive Wīgmundes) + Old English hām ‘village, homestead’.
English: habitational name from Wyndham in Shermanbury (Sussex). The placename derives from an Old English personal name Winda + Old English hamm ‘water meadow’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Wyndham was born in 1501, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Thomas Wyndham, was 33 and her mother, Eleanor Scrope, was 29. She married Andrew Luttrell on 22 April 1514, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 7 July 1580, in East Quantoxhead, Somerset, England, at the age of 79, and was buried in East Quantoxhead, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Wyndham est née en 1501, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas Wyndham, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Scrope, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Andrew Luttrell le 22 avril 1514, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 juillet 1580, à East Quantoxhead, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à East Quantoxhead, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Wyndham 1501 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas Wyndham 33 und ihre Mutter Eleanor Scrope 29. Sie hat am 22. April 1514 in Somerset, England, United Kingdom Andrew Luttrell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. Juli 1580 im Alter von 79 Jahren in East Quantoxhead, Somerset, England und wurde in East Quantoxhead, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Wyndham è nata il nel 1501 nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Thomas Wyndham, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Eleanor Scrope, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Andrew Luttrell il 22 aprile 1514, nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 7 luglio 1580, nel East Quantoxhead, Somerset, England, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolta a East Quantoxhead, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Wyndhamが1501年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Wyndhamは33歳，母Eleanor Scropeは29歳であった。1514年4月22日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトでAndrew Luttrellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1580年7月7日にイギリスソメルセトエアスト クアントクスヘドで79歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトエアスト クアントクスヘドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Wyndham(이)가 1501년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Wyndham(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Scrope(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1514년 4월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 Andrew Luttrell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1580년 7월 7일 영국 서머세트 이스트 퀄토스헤드에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 이스트 퀄토스헤드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Wyndham nasceu em 1501, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Thomas Wyndham, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Scrope, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Andrew Luttrell em 22 de abril de 1514, em Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de julho de 1580, em East Quantoxhead, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 79 anos, e foi sepultada em East Quantoxhead, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Wyndham родилась в 1501 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Wyndham было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Scrope, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Andrew Luttrell 22 апреля 1514 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 7 июля 1580 г. в местности эаст куантоксхид, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности эаст куантоксхид, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Wyndham nació en 1501, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas Wyndham, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Eleanor Scrope, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Andrew Luttrell el 22 de abril de 1514, en Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 7 de julio de 1580, en East Quantoxhead, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultada en East Quantoxhead, Somerset, Inglaterra.
